summary: Matched the F Flower flame item translation unit by replacing the 756-line hand-written assembly file with a 236-line C implementation, moving `itfflowerflame.c` from NonMatching to Matching, wiring the C object into the build, adding the needed item-var struct, and correcting declarations/includes for external users. The PR also localized many internal state callbacks in `symbols.txt` and documented a useful reviewer tactic for reconstructing `ItemStateTable` entries from `.data`.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/it/items/itfflowerflame.s`, removing the old 756-line assembly implementation.

Added a matched C implementation in `src/melee/it/items/itfflowerflame.c` for the F Flower flame item, including spawn/setup logic, animation/state callbacks, per-frame callbacks, and helper wrappers such as `it_8029A748`, `it_8029A89C`, `it_8029A8F4`, and `it_8029B0A8`.
Defined `ItemStateTable it_803F66C8[]` in C with five entries reconstructed from `.data`, using local static callbacks for the table-owned functions.
Added `FFlowerFlameVars` with `Vec3 pos` and `f32 unk` in `itCommonItems.h`, and added `fflowerflame` to the `Item::xDD4_itemVar` union in `it/types.h`.
Changed `configure.py` so `melee/it/items/itfflowerflame.c` is built as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` to link `src/melee/it/items/itfflowerflame.c.o` instead of the assembly object.
Moved public `itfflowerflame` declarations into `itfflowerflame.h`, added needed platform/type includes, and removed `it_8029A89C`/`it_8029A8F4` declarations from the broader `it_27CF.h` header.
Updated `ftcommon.c` to include `it/items/itfflowerflame.h` directly and adjusted the call to `it_8029A8F4` from three arguments to the matched two-argument signature.
Updated `symbols.txt` so many state callbacks from `it_8029AA1C` through `it_8029B074` became `scope:local`, while externally-used functions remained global.
smart_moves: Kept callbacks referenced only by the item state table as `static` in the C file and matched this with `scope:local` in `symbols.txt`, reducing unnecessary header/API exposure.
Reconstructed the state table directly as `ItemStateTable it_803F66C8[]`, preserving the order and function pointer roles from the original `.data` layout.
Introduced a dedicated `FFlowerFlameVars` item-var struct instead of using raw offsets for `xDD4_itemVar`, making repeated position/scale state accesses readable and reusable.
Moved declarations out of a broad item header into `itfflowerflame.h`, then included that specific header from `ftcommon.c`, which is cleaner for dependency ownership.

Used existing item helper calls such as `Item_80268B18`, `Item_80268E5C`, `it_8026B3A8`, `it_80272CC0`, `it_80272F7C`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslate`, and `it_80273130` to express behavior rather than open-coding engine logic.
decomp_lessons: For item files, start by converting the `.data` `ItemStateTable`; its function pointer slots reveal which callbacks exist and often constrain their return types.
Functions that only appear in an item state table do not need public header declarations; make them file-local/static and mark their symbols local.
When a caller in another translation unit needs only a small set of item functions, prefer the item-specific header over a broad catch-all header.
Use a named `xDD4_itemVar` union member when repeated offsets represent a coherent item-specific state block; in this PR the flame item stored a base position and an unknown float.

Special attributes can be ambiguous early in matching. The author noted uncertainty around `xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes`; the merged code used a direct `f32*` cast for the lone member, so future work should revisit if a clearer article-special-attributes struct appears.
Build-system completion for a matched file needs both configure metadata and object-file list changes: `Object(Matching, ...)` plus replacing the `.s.o` object with the `.c.o` object.
naming_conventions: The new item-var struct follows the existing item-specific pattern: `FFlowerFlameVars` next to `FFlowerVars`, with a matching union member named `fflowerflame`.
Unknown fields remained conservatively named, e.g. `FFlowerFlameVars::unk`, rather than inventing semantics not proven by the diff.
Address-based function names were retained (`it_8029A748`, `it_8029AA6C`, etc.), consistent with the current decomp state.
Item-state callback prototypes were annotated with address comments and declared `static` when local to the translation unit.
Public prototypes use project typedefs such as `Item_GObj*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `Vec3*`, `u8`, and `f32`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted `.data` bytes for `it_803F66C8` into five `ItemStateTable` C entries: `{0, callback1, callback2, callback3}` rows, with `NULL` where the original table used null pointers.

Used the position of a function in the `ItemStateTable` to infer callback signature/return type; the review comment specifically notes that a callback such as `it_8029AA6C` should return an integer/bool-like value rather than `u32` based on its slot.

Matched small empty callbacks as empty functions returning either `void` or `false` according to their state-table role, e.g. `it_8029AB60`/`it_8029B070` as empty void callbacks and several bool callbacks returning `false`.
Used `PAD_STACK(4)` in `it_8029A89C`, indicating a stack-shape matching adjustment was needed for that function.
Preserved assembly-observed null checks around item/gobj access in public helpers like `it_8029A89C` and `it_8029A8F4`.
Replaced direct JObj translation copy sequences with `HSD_JObjGetTranslation`/`HSD_JObjSetTranslate` and stored/restored the base position through `xDD4_itemVar.fflowerflame.pos`.

review_feedback.author: wyatt-avilla
review_feedback.feedback: To determine `ItemStateTable` function pointers, look for the corresponding variable in `.data` and translate the sequence of `.4byte` entries into C rows.
review_feedback.reuse: Build the item state table early when decomping an item file; it gives many function declarations and callback return types for free.

review_feedback.author: wyatt-avilla
review_feedback.feedback: The state-table slot can imply return type; the comment cites `it_8029AA6C` as a function that should return an int-like value rather than `u32` based on its table position.
review_feedback.reuse: Use table role before guessing callback prototypes.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked the author to take note of changes in commit `977d3e7b01dea410814ce6852afb31ea0eec70ef` for future contributions.
review_feedback.reuse: Evidence does not include that commit diff, so the exact requested changes are unknown from this slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect commit 977d3e7b01dea410814ce6852afb31ea0eec70ef to identify the specific style or matching corrections ribbanya wanted future contributors to notice.
Search for later PRs that rename `FFlowerFlameVars::unk` or replace the `x4_specialAttributes` `f32*` cast with a typed special-attributes struct.
Compare neighboring item matches that use `ItemStateTable` to confirm consistent callback return types and static/local symbol treatment.
Check whether `itfflowerflame` functions were later renamed beyond address-based names.
